canonical_definition: Benchmark dataset containing controversial yes/no web search queries and labeled support/refute web pages, used to evaluate model performance on debatable query summarization.
papers: CodeSCM: Causal Analysis for Multi-Modal Code Generation

definitions.text: Benchmark dataset containing controversial yes/no web search queries and labeled support/refute web pages, used to evaluate model performance on debatable query summarization.
Dataset of short questions on controversial topics where high-quality responses provide context and elaboration, used to evaluate stance diversity and pluralism in LLM responses.
definitions.paper: CodeSCM: Causal Analysis for Multi-Modal Code Generation

relationships.paper: CodeSCM: Causal Analysis for Multi-Modal Code Generation
relationships.evidence: To measure the semantic diversity of LLMs’ outputs before and after alignment, we chose two datasets containing open-ended questions where a range of valid answers from multiple perspectives exists. CONFLICTINGQA (Wan et al., 2024) consists of short questions on controversial topics. (Section 2.2)

naacl25: CodeSCM: Causal Analysis for Multi-Modal Code Generation

characterization: Benchmark dataset containing controversial yes/no web search queries and labeled support/refute web pages, used to evaluate model performance on debatable query summarization.
